instruction:
We now define an algorithm: The definition of a(n) is the number of positive integers k which are <= n and where k, k-1 and k+1 are each coprime to n. For context, two numbers are considered coprime if their greatest common divisor (GCD) is 1. Given the input x_list (a series of values): [84, 85, 86, 87, 88, 89, 90, 91, 92, 93], determine the corresponding output sequence y_list.

Options:
A. [1, 29, 1, 1, 1, 87, 1, 41, 1, 1]
B. [2, 26, 2, 2, 2, 85, 2, 39, 2, 2]
C. [5, 27, 5, 5, 5, 83, 5, 45, 5, 5]
D. [0, 28, 0, 0, 0, 86, 0, 40, 0, 0]
E. [4, 30, 4, 4, 4, 88, 4, 37, 4, 4]
F. [8, 32, 8, 8, 8, 82, 8, 36, 8, 8]
G. [6, 31, 6, 6, 6, 89, 6, 38, 6, 6]
H. [9, 33, 9, 9, 9, 91, 9, 44, 9, 9]
I. [3, 25, 3, 3, 3, 84, 3, 42, 3, 3]
J. [7, 24, 7, 7, 7, 90, 7, 43, 7, 7]
